debug_info.goal:
C : Type
inst✝¹ : Category.{0, 0} C
K : Type
inst✝ : DivisionRing K
M : CategoryTheory.Functor C (ModuleCat K)
D : @Dual.DirectSumDecomposition C inst✝¹ K inst✝ M
B : (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) → N ∈ D → Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
hB : ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ∈ D), N = sSup (B N hN)
hB' : ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ∈ D), sSupIndep (B N hN)
hB'' : ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ∈ D), ⊥ ∉ B N hN
⊢ @Dual.DirectSumDecomposition.refinement C inst✝¹ K inst✝ M D B hB hB' hB'' ≤ D

failed_attempts:
by
  refine' fun N hN => _
  simp only [refinement] at hN ⊢
  -- We need to find a subset of D whose sSup is N.
  -- Since N is in the refinement, it is in B N' hN' for some N' in D.
  -- We can use N' as the subset.
  have hN' : ∃ (N' : PersistenceSubmodule M), N' ∈ D ∧ N ∈ B N' hN' := by
    simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, exists_prop] at hN
    obtain ⟨N', hN', N'', hN''⟩ := hN
    refine' ⟨N', hN', _⟩
    simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion]
    <;> aesop
  obtain ⟨N', hN', hN''⟩ := hN'
  have h₁ : N' ∈ D := hN'
  refine' ⟨{N'}, _⟩
  -- Now we need to show that N = sSup {N} and {N} ⊆ D.
  constructor
  · -- Show that {N'} ⊆ D.
    intro x hx
    simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hx
    rw [hx]
    exact h₁
  · -- Show that N = sSup {N'}.
    have h₂ : N ≤ N' := by
      -- Since N ∈ B N' hN' and B N' hN' is independent, N ≤ sSup B N' hN' = N'.
      have h₃ : sSup (B N' h₁) = N' := by
        rw [hB N' h₁]
      have h₄ : N ≤ sSup (B N' h₁) := by
        apply le_sSup_of_le N
        · exact hN''
      rw [h₃] at h₄
      exact h₄
    have h₃ : N' ≤ N := by
      -- Since N' = sSup B N' hN' and B N' hN' is independent, N' ≤ sSup {N} = N.
      have h₄ : N' = sSup (B N' h₁) := by
        rw [hB N' h₁]
      have h₅ : sSup (B N' h₁) ≤ N := by
        -- Each element of B N' h₁ is ≤ N because B N' h₁ refines {N}.
        have h₆ : ∀ P ∈ B N' h₁, P ≤ N := by
          intro P hP
          have h₇ : P ≤ sSup (B N' h₁) := le_sSup hP
          have h₈ : sSup (B N' h₁) ≤ N := by
            have h₉ : sSup (B N' h₁) = N' := by
              rw [hB N' h₁]
            rw [h₉]
            exact h₂
          exact le_trans h₇ h₈
        exact sSup_le h₆
      rw [h₄] at *
      exact h₅
    -- Therefore, N = N'.
    exact le_antisymm h₃ h₂

  intro N hN
  refine' ⟨B N hN, _, _⟩
  · -- Prove that B N hN ⊆ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier
    intro X hX
    simp only [refinement, Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at *
    refine' ⟨N, ⟨hN, X, hX⟩, rfl⟩
  · -- Prove that N = sSup (B N hN)

by
  intro N hN
  have h₁ : N ∈ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier := hN
  have h₂ : N ∈ D := by
    have h₃ : N ∈ (⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN) := h₁
    -- If N is in the union of all B N hN, then it must be in some B N hN for some N ∈ D.
    rcases h₃ with ⟨N₀, hN₀, N₁, hN₁⟩
    have h₄ : N = N₁ := by
      -- Since N is in B N₀ hN₀, and B N₀ hN₀ is a subset of D, we have N ∈ D.
      simp_all [DirectSumDecomposition.refinement]
    rw [h₄]
    -- Now we need to show that N₁ ∈ D.
    -- Since N₁ ∈ B N₀ hN₀, and B N₀ hN₀ is a subset of D, we have N₁ ∈ D.
    have h₅ : N₁ ∈ B N₀ hN₀ := by simp_all
    have h₆ : B N₀ hN₀ ⊆ {M | M ∈ D} := by
      -- This is because B N₀ hN₀ is a subset of D.
      have h₇ : ∀ M ∈ B N₀ hN₀, M ∈ D := by
        intro M hM
        have h₈ : M ∈ (⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN) := by
          -- Since M ∈ B N₀ hN₀, and B N₀ hN₀ is a subset of the union, we have M in the union.
          simp_all [DirectSumDecomposition.refinement]
          <;> aesop
        simp_all [DirectSumDecomposition.refinement]
        <;> aesop
      intro M hM
      simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at h₇ ⊢
      exact h₇ M hM
    have h₇ : N₁ ∈ {M | M ∈ D} := h₆ h₅
    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at h₇
    exact h₇
  -- Finally, we have N ∈ D, as required.
  exact h₂

by
  have h_main : ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D → ∃ (S : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), S ⊆ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier ∧ N = sSup S := by
    intro N hN
    have h₁ : N = sSup (B N hN) := hB N hN
    have h₂ : (B N hN) ⊆ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier := by
      intro x hx
      simp only [DirectSumDecomposition.refinement.carrier, Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      exact ⟨N, hN, hx⟩
    refine ⟨B N hN, h₂, _⟩
    rw [h₁]
  
  have h_final : refinement B hB hB' hB'' ≤ D := by
    intro N hN
    have h₁ : ∃ (S : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), S ⊆ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier ∧ N = sSup S := h_main (hN := hN)
    obtain ⟨S, hS_sub, hS_eq⟩ := h₁
    refine ⟨S, _, _⟩
    · exact hS_sub
    · exact hS_eq
  
  exact h_final

by
  have h_main : ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M), N ∈ D → ∃ (B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), B ⊆ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier ∧ N = sSup B := by
    intro N hN
    have h₁ : N ∈ D := hN
    have h₂ : N = sSup (B N h₁) := hB N h₁
    have h₃ : sSupIndep (B N h₁) := hB' N h₁
    have h₄ : ⊥ ∉ B N h₁ := hB'' N h₁
    refine' ⟨B N h₁, _, _⟩
    · -- Prove that B N h₁ is a subset of the carrier of the refinement
      intro x hx
      have h₅ : x ∈ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier := by
        simp only [refinement, Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion] at *
        refine' ⟨N, h₁, _⟩
        exact ⟨hx, by simp_all⟩
      exact h₅
    · -- Prove that the supremum of B N h₁ is N
      exact h₂
  
  refine' fun N hN => ⟨_, _⟩
  · -- Existence of a finite cover for N ∈ D
    have h₁ := h_main N hN
    obtain ⟨B, hB_subset, hB_eq⟩ := h₁
    refine' ⟨B, fun B => by aesop, _⟩
    <;> simp_all [hB_eq]
  · -- The refinement condition for N ∈ D
    intro B hB
    have h₁ : B ⊆ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier := hB
    have h₂ : sSup B = sSup ((refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier) := by
      apply le_antisymm
      · -- sSup B ≤ sSup ((refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier)
        apply sSup_le_sSup_of_le
        · intro x hx
          exact h₁ hx
        · intro x hx
          simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion]
          <;> aesop
      · -- sSup ((refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier) ≤ sSup B
        apply sSup_le_sSup_of_le
        · intro x hx
          simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion]
          <;> aesop
        · intro x hx
          exact hx
    have h₃ : sSup ((refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier) = ⊤ := by
      have h₄ : sSup ((refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier) = ⊤ := by
        -- Use the fact that the carrier of the refinement is a superset of D's carrier
        have h₅ : D.carrier ⊆ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier := by
          intro x hx
          simp only [refinement, Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion] at *
          refine' ⟨x, hx, _⟩
          have h₆ : x = sSup (B x hx) := hB x hx
          aesop
        -- Use the property of sSup_eq_top for the refinement
        have h₆ : sSup ((refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier) = ⊤ := by
          have h₇ : sSup D.carrier = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
          have h₈ : sSup ((refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier) ≥ sSup D.carrier := by
            apply sSup_mono h₅
          calc
            sSup ((refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier) ≥ sSup D.carrier := h₈
            _ = ⊤ := h₇
        exact h₆
      exact h₄
    -- Combine the results to get the final equality
    calc
      sSup B = sSup ((refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier) := h₂
      _ = ⊤ := h₃

  intro N hN
  have h₁ : N ∈ D := hN
  classical
  have h₂ : ∃ (h : N ∈ D), True := ⟨h₁, by trivial⟩
  -- Choose a proof `hN' : N ∈ D`, which is the same as `h₁` (but using classical choice)
  obtain ⟨hN', _⟩ := h₂
  -- Use `B N hN'` as the subset of the refinement
  use (B N hN')
  constructor
  · -- Prove `B N hN' ⊆ carrier (refinement B hB hB' hB'')`
    intro C hC

    refine' ⟨N, h₁, hN', _⟩
    exact hC
  · -- Prove `sSup (B N hN') = N`
    rw [hB N hN']
    <;> simp

by
  intro N hN
  have h₁ : N ∈ D := by
    have h₂ : ∃ (Z : S), N ≤ Z := by
      have h₃ : N = sSup (B N hx) := hB N hx
      have h₄ : sSup (B N hx) ≤ sSup D := by
        apply sSup_le_sSup
        intro p hp
        have h₅ : p ∈ ⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN := by
          -- Prove that p is in the union of B N hN for all N in D and hN
          simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion] at hp ⊢
          aesop
        simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion] at h₅
        simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe]
        <;> aesop
      have h₅ : sSup (B N hx) ≤ sSup D := h₄
      have h₆ : N ≤ sSup D := by
        rw [h₃]
        exact h₅
      have h₇ : N ≤ sSup D := h₆
      have h₈ : sSup D = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
      have h₉ : N ≤ ⊤ := by
        calc
          N ≤ sSup D := h₇
          _ = ⊤ := h₈
      -- Use the fact that N ≤ ⊤ to find a Z in D such that N ≤ Z
      have h₁₀ : ∃ (Z : D), N ≤ Z.val := by
        classical
        by_contra h
        push_neg at h
        -- If no such Z exists, then N is a subset of the complement of D
        have h₁₁ : N ⊆ sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))ᶜ := by
          intro x hx
          have h₁₂ : x ∉ sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
            intro hx'
            have h₁₃ : ∃ (Z : D), x ∈ Z.val := by
              simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion] at hx'
              aesop
            obtain ⟨Z, hZ⟩ := h₁₃
            have h₁₄ : N ≤ Z.val := by
              have h₁₅ := h Z
              simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe]
              <;> aesop
            have h₁₆ : x ∈ N := hx
            have h₁₇ : x ∈ Z.val := hZ
            aesop
          exact h₁₂
        have h₁₂ : N = ⊥ := by
          -- Prove that N must be the bottom element
          apply eq_bot_iff.mpr
          intro x hx
          have h₁₃ : x ∈ N := hx
          have h₁₄ : x ∈ sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))ᶜ := h₁₁ h₁₃
          simp only [Set.mem_compl_iff, Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at h₁₄
          have h₁₅ : ¬(∃ (Z : D), x ∈ Z.val) := by tauto
          have h₁₆ : x = 0 := by
            by_contra h₁₇
            -- If x ≠ 0, then there exists a Z in D such that x ∈ Z.val
            have h₁₈ : ∃ (Z : D), x ∈ Z.val := by
              -- Use the fact that x ≠ 0 to find such a Z
              have h₁₉ : ∃ (Z : D), x ∈ Z.val := by
                classical
                -- Use the Axiom of Choice to find such a Z
                by_contra h₂₀
                push_neg at h₂₀
                have h₂₁ : ∀ (Z : D), x ∉ Z.val := by tauto
                have h₂₂ : x ∉ sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
                  intro h₂₃
                  have h₂₄ : ∃ (Z : D), x ∈ Z.val := by
                    simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion] at h₂₃
                    aesop
                  obtain ⟨Z, hZ⟩ := h₂₄
                  have h₂₅ : x ∈ Z.val := hZ
                  have h₂₆ : x ∉ Z.val := h₂₁ Z
                  contradiction
                have h₂₇ : x ∈ sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
                  have h₂₈ : sSup (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
                  have h₂₉ : x ∈ (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
                    trivial
                  simpa [h₂₈] using h₂₉
                contradiction
              exact h₁₉
            obtain ⟨Z, hZ⟩ := h₁₈
            have h₂₀ : x ∈ Z.val := hZ
            have h₂₁ : ∃ (Z : D), x ∈ Z.val := ⟨Z, hZ⟩
            tauto
          simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe]
          <;> aesop
        have h₁₃ : N = ⊥ := h₁₂
        have h₁₄ : ⊥ ∉ (D : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := D.not_bot_mem'
        simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe]
        <;> aesop
      obtain ⟨Z, hZ⟩ := h₁₀
      refine' ⟨Z, _⟩
      simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe]
      <;> aesop
    obtain ⟨Z, hZ⟩ := h₂
    have h₃ : N ≤ Z := hZ
    have h₄ : Z ∈ D := by
      simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe]
      <;> aesop
    have h₅ : N ∈ D := by
      simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₅
  exact h₁

  have h_main : ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M), N ∈ D → ∃ (C : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), C ⊆ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier ∧ N = sSup C := by
    intro N hN

    constructor
    · -- Prove that B N hN ⊆ (refinement B hB hB' hB'').carrier
      intro x hx
      simp only [refinement.carrier, Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_iUnion] at *
      -- Since x ∈ B N hN, we can find N' = N and hN' = hN such that x ∈ B N' hN'

  -- Use the main lemma to show that the refinement is indeed a refinement of D

by
  intro N hN
  -- We need to show that for each N in the refined decomposition, there exists a subset of D whose supremum is N.
  have h₁ : N ∈ D ∨ ∃ (P : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hP : P ∈ D) (hP' : N ∈ B P hP), True := by
    -- Check if N is in D or if it belongs to some B P hP
    have h₂ : N ∈ ⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN := hN
    simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, exists_prop] at h₂
    -- If N is in D, we are done. Otherwise, it must be in some B P hP
    by_cases h₃ : N ∈ D
    · exact Or.inl h₃
    · exact Or.inr (by aesop)
  cases h₁ with
  | inl h₁ =>
    -- If N is in D, we can take {N} as the subset of D whose supremum is N
    use {N}
    constructor
    · -- Prove that {N} is a subset of D
      intro x hx
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hx
      rw [hx]
      exact h₁
    · -- Prove that the supremum of {N} is N
      simp [h₁]
  | inr h₁ =>
    -- If N is in B P hP for some P in D, then N is less than or equal to P
    rcases h₁ with ⟨P, hP, hP', _⟩
    -- Since N is in B P hP and P is in D, we can use the fact that B P hP is a subset of the refined decomposition
    have h₂ : N ≤ P := by
      have h₃ : N ∈ B P hP := hP'
      have h₄ : P = sSup (B P hP) := hB P hP
      have h₅ : sSupIndep (B P hP) := hB' P hP
      have h₆ : N ≤ sSup (B P hP) := le_sSup h₃
      linarith
    -- We can take {P} as the subset of D whose supremum is P, and since N ≤ P, we have N ≤ sSup {P}
    use {P}
    constructor
    · -- Prove that {P} is a subset of D
      intro x hx
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hx
      rw [hx]
      exact hP
    · -- Prove that the supremum of {P} is P
      have h₃ : P = sSup (B P hP) := hB P hP
      have h₄ : N ≤ P := h₂
      have h₅ : sSup ({P} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) = P := by
        simp [sSup_singleton]
      have h₆ : N ≤ sSup ({P} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
        rw [h₅]
        exact h₄
      have h₇ : N = sSup ({P} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
        have h₈ : sSup ({P} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) ≤ N := by
          have h₉ : N ≤ P := h₂
          have h₁₀ : sSup ({P} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) = P := by
            simp [sSup_singleton]
          rw [h₁₀]
          <;> simp_all [sSup_singleton]
          <;> aesop
        have h₁₁ : N ≤ sSup ({P} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := h₆
        have h₁₂ : sSup ({P} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) ≤ N := h₈
        exact le_antisymm h₁₁ h₁₂
      simp_all [sSup_singleton]
      <;> aesop
